architecture_fit.note: Minimal delta from base_model: one tempered scalar on K@q, single attention head, no MLP; canonical alignment 0.9996 solves the retrieval task.

baseline_comparison.note: Three explicit strawmen under identical conditions: raw K@q (~0.19), temperature=0 ablation (uniform 1/L), random logits — method clearly succeeds where they fail.

faithfulness.note: temperature=0 ablation collapses behaviour to uniform, isolating the tempered K@q circuit; synthetic nature stated and trained-model equivalent proposed, though the knockout is near-tautological.

operating_range.note: Full interference sweep (cos 0→1) with the breakdown located: graceful decay to the 0.5 split at cos=1; goal's relevant axis fully covered, no silent failure.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Entirely hand-set circuit (no training); mechanism written out by hand as beta*(K@q), reproducing target behaviour.

visual_judgement.note: Alignment-vs-interference curve with all variants + uniform baseline + ceiling line, plus a per-slice target/distractor/other mass-split bar — claim is legible.

visualisation_rationale.note: README ties y-axis (target mass), x-axis (distractor cosine), the strawman overlays and the 0.5 ceiling annotation directly to the goal's question.

notes: A disciplined hand-built attempt: one temperature delta from base_model attention, a single MLP-free head, reaching ~1.0 canonical alignment with clear strawmen (raw K@q, temperature=0 ablation, random) and a temperature-zero knockout for causality. The headline robustness of 0.5 is correctly explained as the physical ceiling at cos=1 (distractor identical to target), and the visualisation makes both the success and the ceiling legible. Mean of scored items ~4.7 → good.
